---
title: "symmetric_degree_vector"
generated: true
description: "Degree of the symmetrised adjacency $W =  tfrac{1}{2}(A + A^{ top})$."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.graph.laplacian`](../nitrix-graph-laplacian.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:symmetric_degree_vector -->
```python
symmetric_degree_vector(A: _GraphInput) -> Num[Array, '... n']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:symmetric_degree_vector -->
Degree of the symmetrised adjacency $W = \tfrac{1}{2}(A + A^{\top})$.

The per-node degree is
$d_i = \tfrac{1}{2}(\sum_j A_{ij} + \sum_j A_{ji}) =
\tfrac{1}{2}(\text{out-degree} + \text{in-degree})$ -- the degree the
symmetric normalised Laplacian / affinity must normalise by when `A`
is **not** symmetric (e.g. a top-k-per-row sparsified affinity).
Normalising by the bare row-sum out-degree instead uses the wrong
diagonal, so $\tfrac{1}{2}(D^{-1/2} A D^{-1/2} + (\cdot)^{\top})$
is no longer $D_W^{-1/2} W D_W^{-1/2}$ and its trivial (constant)
eigenvalue drifts off $1$ -- the implicit "symmetrise the
Laplacian, not the adjacency" error.  Equals [`degree_vector`](../nitrix-graph-laplacian/degree_vector.qmd)
exactly for symmetric `A`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:symmetric_degree_vector -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `A` | `_GraphInput` | — | — | Adjacency operator: dense `(..., n, n)`, [`ELL`](../nitrix-sparse.qmd), or [`SectionedELL`](../nitrix-sparse.qmd). |

**Returns** `Num` — shape `...` × `n`. Per-node degree of the symmetrised adjacency, shape `(..., n)`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:symmetric_degree_vector -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
